notes: Verified with gh on 2026-07-13. Lumenloop API/content-pipeline issues belong in lumenloop/lumenloop-backend; directory-record corrections belong in lumenloop/stellar-ecosystem-db.
Algolia ranking-only docs findings may not have a clear public intake even though developers.stellar.org content lives in stellar/stellar-docs.

default.rule: Target the source repository that owns the referenced skill; never file against this repo's mirror.

stellar-light-scout.alsoAvailable: Stellar-Light/stellarlight owns API/data/discovery behavior; Stellar-Light/stellar-scout owns the Scout skill; Stellar-Light/scout-mcp owns the MCP wrapper. The API also exposes keyless POST /api/feedback.

stellar-docs.contentRule: developers.stellar.org content and content-structure findings file in stellar/stellar-docs.
stellar-docs.rankingRule: Pure Algolia ranking/tokenization findings may need docs/search-owner triage; record intake unclear per finding when the repo cannot plausibly own the search behavior.

lumenloop.rule: File API and content-pipeline findings in lumenloop/lumenloop-backend. Use lumenloop/stellar-ecosystem-db only for committed directory-record corrections.

sk-009.reason: The finding concerns the upstream Scout skill API reference, not the live service implementation.

ll-008.reason: Committed directory-record coverage and region correction.

sd-001.reason: Algolia ranking/tokenization behavior; not clearly owned by stellar/stellar-docs issue intake.

sd-002.reason: Ranking-only finding and currently proposed/possibly fixed.

sd-007.reason: Docs content ambiguity; in-charter per README.

sd-008.reason: Official Software Versions content lags live Mainnet activation and stable release feeds.

sd-009.reason: Developer docs omit the canonical SEP-6 interactive-component deprecation status.

sd-010.reason: The Indexers page describes Alchemy's Stellar Data API as a future launch after the provider's official indexed-data product became live.

sd-011.reason: Current SDEX documentation can lead readers to rely on unused offer lifecycle effects instead of transaction result XDR and current-state resources.

sls-020.reason: Current security-program migration and supersession facts are absent from research retrieval, allowing stale SDF landing content to double-count or misroute disclosure programs.

sls-023.reason: Broad RWA discovery exposes entity-level status without product-level Stellar deployment state, allowing planned DTCC work and unverified/tooling rows to be presented as live tokenized products.

sls-024.reason: Project lifecycle labels do not expose source, as-of date, or entity-versus-deployment scope when current operator surfaces conflict.

sls-029.reason: Oracle discovery collapses provider, product family, network, contract evidence, and announcement status into one Live label.

sls-033.reason: Wallet enumeration mixes records, products, aliases, and availability states, so exact wallet counts and platform claims are unsafe.

sls-036.reason: Leaderboard activity semantics, source timestamps, and DeFi filters are insufficient to distinguish activity from commits, stars, issues, TVL, or usage.

sls-039.reason: Project TVL rows expose a point but no history/method URL or metric decomposition for current-versus-trend/peak/record questions.

sd-012.reason: Official Lumens documentation retains older SDF wallet labels that conflict with the current mandate taxonomy and account mapping.

sd-013.reason: Account creation and merge docs omit sponsored zero-balance creation plus signer/sponsor merge asymmetry present in CAP-0033 and current Core.

sd-014.reason: Ledger, transaction-set, result, archive, and SCP documentation lacks one explicit transaction-inclusion verification recipe and trust-boundary explanation.

sd-015.reason: The Starlight glossary lacks the archived/experimental lifecycle qualifier needed to prevent a historical prototype from being presented as maintained production L2 infrastructure.

sd-016.reason: Hand-maintained network capacity scalars differ from live CONFIG_SETTING and ledger fields; current tables need live-derived, dated values.

sd-017.reason: Official Docs disagree on whether Horizon is deprecated versus nearing EOL/not yet deprecated, despite continuing endpoint availability.

sd-018.reason: Token-interface docs omit the current SAC/CAP-67 asset-appended event schema, leading direct SAC events to be misclassified as generic SEP-41.

sd-019.reason: The TTL extension guide incorrectly says one ExtendFootprintTTLOp cannot cover multiple read-only footprint entries.

sd-020.reason: SAC docs retain obsolete XDR/executable terminology and omit executable-based identity, public name metadata, and mutable-admin boundaries.

sd-021.reason: P25 BN254 links the wrong CAP and ZK docs blur host permutations, raw feature-gated SDK APIs, and high-level Poseidon hashing.

sd-022.reason: Introductory no_std wording makes the default no-allocator configuration sound like an absolute despite current SDK alloc support.

sd-023.reason: Static getEvents day-count retention guidance conflicts with current stellar-rpc configuration and live getHealth.

sd-024.reason: Transaction-resource prose retains older field names and blurs validation/preflight/apply-time enforcement and transaction-vs-ledger limits.

sd-025.reason: The Horizon-to-RPC migration guide still describes CAP-67 unified asset events as near-future despite Protocol 23 finality and live Protocol 27.

sd-026.reason: Contract-build docs still demonstrate the standalone optimize command even though CLI 27 deprecates it and optimizes builds by default.

sd-027.reason: The current Relayer page says LaunchTube is discontinued while the published Guestbook passkey prerequisites still require a LaunchTube JWT without a legacy/migration warning.

sd-028.reason: Official Horizon admin pages conflict on whether HISTORY_RETENTION_COUNT defaults to 0/unlimited or 518400, while v27.0.0 source resolves the executable default to 0.

sd-029.reason: Current RPC operator docs omit the shipped v25.1+ BACKFILL startup path, prerequisites, and getLedgers-only direct datastore boundary.

sd-030.reason: Lab docs do not make plaintext Saved Keypairs localStorage and the self-hosted-versus-air-gap boundary prominent enough for secret-bearing workflows.

sd-031.reason: Current Lab/Quickstart docs and translations still teach the removed stellar network container start command.

sd-032.reason: Current Flutter wallet tutorial dependency ranges are stale and can conflict with the current wallet SDK dependency graph.

sd-033.reason: The Client SDKs page describes the current Python SDK as Horizon-only despite shipped and documented Stellar RPC support.

sd-034.reason: The current smart-wallet guide routes greenfield readers to legacy Passkey Kit without carrying its upstream unaudited-demo and no-real-value warning or its Smart Account Kit successor.

sd-035.reason: The Wallets Kit docs reference the legacy dotted npm scope and stale API while the maintainer now prefers the v2 JSR scope and static initialization model.

sk-010.reason: The official dapp skill embeds the legacy Wallets Kit npm scope and v1 constructor/allowAllModules API instead of the current v2 JSR/static-init path.

sd-036.reason: CAP-0075's protocol-version and field-selector contradictions are owned by the normative stellar-protocol source, distinct from sd-021's Stellar Docs errors.

sd-037.reason: SLP proposal-family discoverability is rooted in the normative stellar-protocol limits/ source and needs canonical family metadata/cross-links.

sk-011.reason: The served upstream zk-proofs skill needs Protocol 25 status correction.

sk-012.reason: The served upstream agentic-payments skill needs current MPP Session terminology.

sls-055.reason: Scout research omits canonical non-blog SDF organizational pages, leaving current legal-structure, fund-scope, mandate, leadership, and report claims without quotable coverage.
